users.description: One row per user. MVP only ever has one user (seeded), created via login with hardcoded credentials, but the schema supports many.

boards.description: One row per board. MVP: exactly one board per user.

columns.user_id: INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

kanban_columns.description: A column on a board (e.g. Backlog, In Progress). Named kanban_columns to avoid clashing with the SQL term 'column'.

columns.board_id: INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES boards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
columns.title: TEXT NOT NULL

kanban_columns.notes: position determines left-to-right column order on the board, 0-based.

columns.column_id: INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES kanban_columns(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
columns.title: TEXT NOT NULL

cards.notes: position determines top-to-bottom card order within its column, 0-based. Moving a card between columns updates column_id and re-numbers position in both the source and destination columns.
